description: Unique identifier assigned to each project in the knowledge graph. Essential for referencing specific projects across all queries. Used as primary key for data integrity and audit trails.
sample_prompt: List all Project IDs from the dataset.
variation_in_prompt: Show all project codes; List unique project identifiers; What projects exist? | Can you give me the ID numbers of projects in your database?
assumption_in_prompt: IDs are unique and non-null. Format is standardized (numeric). No duplicate IDs exist in the system.

description: Official registered name of the real estate project as per RERA documentation. This is the marketing name used by developers and essential for project identification in reports and communications.
sample_prompt: List the names of the projects.
variation_in_prompt: List all project names; Show me the projects you have; What are the project names? | Give me the list of all projects in your database with their names.
assumption_in_prompt: Project names are official and standardized. Names match RERA registration documents. No special characters issues.

description: Legal name of the developer/builder who is executing the project. Includes registered company name and is critical for partner analysis, reputation tracking, and relationship management in the real estate portfolio.
variation_in_prompt: Who are the builders? Show developer names; List all developers; Which companies are building these projects? | Can you tell me which developers are in your portfolio?
assumption_in_prompt: Developer names are standardized and match company registration. One developer per project primary assignment. Historical developer changes are noted separately.

description: Geographical micro-market where the project is located. Standardized location name used for market segmentation, competitor analysis, and location-based price benchmarking. Crucial for market comparisons.
variation_in_prompt: Which locations are covered? Show all micro-markets; Where are projects located? | List locations of all projects. What areas do you cover?
assumption_in_prompt: Location names are standardized (standardized micro-market names). Each project maps to exactly one location. Location boundaries are consistent across queries.

description: Official project launch date when the project became available for sales. This temporal marker is essential for market entry analysis, project lifecycle tracking, and competitive positioning assessment.
variation_in_prompt: When did projects launch? Show launch dates; When were projects started for sale? | Can you tell me when each project started selling units?
assumption_in_prompt: Date format is MM-YYYY (consistent). Launch date = first sales opening date. Before this date, no sales were recorded.

description: Expected or actual project handover date as per RERA registration. Represents when buyers will receive possession of units. Critical for timeline planning, investor returns calculation, and project status tracking.
variation_in_prompt: When will projects be delivered? Show possession dates; Expected delivery timeline? | Tell me when units will be ready for handover.
assumption_in_prompt: Date format is MM-YYYY. Based on RERA registrations (official dates). Represents expected delivery, not actual if project is ongoing.

description: Total number of housing units in the entire project (all phases combined for multi-phase developments). This represents total market supply and is fundamental for market penetration and absorption rate calculations.
sample_prompt:
What is the project size of X project ?  
What is the Avg project size in Y location ?
variation_in_prompt: How many units in total? Total project size? How many apartments/homes? | What is the total supply of units in each project?
assumption_in_prompt: Size includes all marketable units. Multi-phase projects: all units included. Parking, commercial spaces excluded from count.

description: Total number of units that have been launched and made available for sale (marketable supply). May differ from Project Size if phased launch is occurring. Used for market penetration and inventory calculations.
variation_in_prompt: What is total supply? How many units launched? Units in market? | Tell me how many units are available for sale in each project.
assumption_in_prompt: Supply = Launched units. Includes open units only (not yet sold). Does not include pre-launch or future phases.

description: Percentage of total launched supply that has been sold (units booked/completed). Key metric for market absorption and investor confidence. Calculated as (Sold Units / Total Supply) × 100.
variation_in_prompt: What % is sold? Show sell-through rates; How much has sold? | What percentage of units have been sold in each project?
assumption_in_prompt: Sold% = Booked + Completed units only. Does not include pre-booking. Represents market response and project attractiveness.

description: Percentage of total supply remaining unsold (available for purchase). Represents market opportunity and risk factor. Calculated as (Unsold Units / Total Supply) × 100. Complements Sold%.
variation_in_prompt: What % remains? Show remaining inventory; How much is unsold? | Tell me what percentage of units are still available in each project.
assumption_in_prompt: Unsold% = 100% - Sold%. Represents current market inventory. Does not include withdrawn units or held inventory.

description: Units sold in last 12 months.
variation_in_prompt: How many units sold annually? Last 12 months sales? Yearly sales units? | Tell me the total units sold in the last 12 months.
assumption_in_prompt: 12-month period.

description: Annual revenue.
variation_in_prompt: What is annual revenue? Last year revenue? Yearly sales value? | Tell me the total revenue collected in the last 12 months.
assumption_in_prompt: Value in Crores.

description: Average saleable area per unit measured in square feet. Includes carpet area plus proportionate common area. Fundamental for price normalization (PSF calculations) and buyer preference analysis across unit types.
variation_in_prompt: What is average unit size? How big are the units? Average area per apartment? | Tell me the average saleable area of units in each project.
assumption_in_prompt: Size = Saleable area (includes common area proportionate share). Represents average across all unit types (1BHK, 2BHK, etc.). Weighted by unit count if varied.

description: Launch price per sqft.
variation_in_prompt: Initial PSF
assumption_in_prompt: Launch-time price.

description: Current saleable PSF.
variation_in_prompt: Market PSF
assumption_in_prompt: Latest price.

description: Percentage of total supply absorbed per month (monthly absorption rate). Indicates market pace and demand intensity. Critical for timeline projections and market heat assessment.
variation_in_prompt: What is monthly absorption rate? How fast are units selling? Monthly sales %? | Tell me what percentage of units sell each month on average.
assumption_in_prompt: Velocity% applied uniformly on Total Supply. Based on last 12-month trend. Assumes no significant seasonal variation going forward.

description: Regulatory compliance indicator showing if the project is registered under Real Estate Regulation Act (RERA). Mandatory for all residential projects in India. Critical for legal and investment risk assessment.
variation_in_prompt: Is it RERA registered? Compliance status? Legal approval status? | Tell me which projects are registered with RERA for legal compliance.
assumption_in_prompt: Y = Registered with RERA authority. N = Not registered (high risk). Data reflects current status. Registration status is verified.

description: Absolute count of units remaining unsold (still available for purchase). Calculated from Total Supply and Unsold%. Represents current market inventory. Critical for absorption and time-to-sell calculations.
variation_in_prompt: How many units left? Remaining inventory count? Available units? | Tell me how many units are still unsold in each project.
assumption_in_prompt: Unsold = Total Supply × Unsold%. Formula accuracy depends on Unsold% precision. Represents current state at snapshot date.

description: Total count of units sold to date (booked or completed). Derived from Total Supply and Sold%. Represents market absorption progress. Used in revenue calculations and performance tracking.
variation_in_prompt: How many sold? Total sales count? How many have been purchased? | Tell me the total number of units sold in each project.
assumption_in_prompt: Sold = Total Supply × Sold%. Includes only confirmed bookings/completed sales. Excludes cancellations (net of returns).

description: Monthly absorption from annual sales.
variation_in_prompt: Monthly absorption
assumption_in_prompt: Annual Sales = last 12 months.

description: Units sold per month from velocity %.
variation_in_prompt: Actual monthly sales
assumption_in_prompt: Velocity% applies on total supply.

description: Time to exhaust unsold stock.
variation_in_prompt: Inventory months
assumption_in_prompt: Absorption steady.

description: % growth since launch.
variation_in_prompt: % appreciation
assumption_in_prompt: Launch PSF accurate.

description: Actual achieved PSF.
variation_in_prompt: Achieved PSF
assumption_in_prompt: Revenue accurate.

description: Average revenue per sold unit.
variation_in_prompt: avg ticket
assumption_in_prompt: Annual revenue accurate.

description: Value of remaining stock.
variation_in_prompt: stock valuation
assumption_in_prompt: PSF×Area holds constant.

description: % of total supply sold annually.
variation_in_prompt: sell-through ratio
assumption_in_prompt: Annual period = 12 months.

description: Projected time required to sell remaining unsold units at current absorption rate. Indicates market demand strength and project runway. Calculated as Unsold Units / Annual Sales rate.
sample_prompt: How long to sell remaining stock?
variation_in_prompt: How long to sell remaining? Months to clear inventory? Project runway? | Tell me how long it will take to sell all remaining units.
assumption_in_prompt: Based on last 12-month sales pace (assumed constant). Excludes seasonality impact. Assumes no marketing intensity changes.

description: Average price per unit at current rates.
variation_in_prompt: unit pricing
assumption_in_prompt: Uses current PSF.

description: Price per unit at launch.
variation_in_prompt: launch pricing
assumption_in_prompt: Uses launch PSF.

description: Difference between current and launch PSF.
variation_in_prompt: pricing delta
assumption_in_prompt: Both PSF values accurate.

description: Current lifecycle stage of the project (Pre-Launch, Ongoing Sales, Nearing Completion, Ready to Occupy, Completed/Possession Ongoing). Indicates project maturity and investment risk level. Essential for portfolio segmentation and market positioning.
sample_prompt: What is the current status of the projects?
variation_in_prompt: Show project statuses; Which projects are under construction? Which are selling now? Which are completed? | Tell me the current stage of each project (selling, completed, etc.)
assumption_in_prompt: Status reflects current project phase. Updated quarterly or after major milestone. Status classifications are standardized.
sample_values: Ongoing Sales, Ready to Occupy, Completed
expected_answer: Ongoing Sales, Ready to Occupy, Completed

description: Duration in months since official project launch to current date. Indicates project maturity in market. Used for cohort analysis, market response benchmarking, and lifecycle stage assessment.
sample_prompt: How old are the projects (in months from launch)?
variation_in_prompt: Show project age; How many months since launch? How long have projects been in market? | Tell me how long each project has been selling (age in months).
assumption_in_prompt: Age calculated from Launch Date to today. Represents time in market (from first sales). Updated monthly.

description: Remaining time from today until expected possession date. Indicates buyer timeline and project completion stage. Critical for investment return calculations and buyer decision-making.
sample_prompt: How many months until possession for each project?
variation_in_prompt: Show time to delivery; When will possession happen? How long until handover? | Tell me how many months from now until buyers get their units.
assumption_in_prompt: Calculated as (Possession Date - Current Date) / 30. Assumes RERA dates are accurate. Positive = future, Negative = overdue.

description: Total development/construction cost of the entire project estimated or actual. Fundamental for developer profitability analysis, ROI calculations, and capital requirements assessment. Includes land + construction.
sample_prompt: What is the total project cost?
variation_in_prompt: Show total development cost; How much was spent building? Project investment amount? | Tell me the total cost to develop each project.
assumption_in_prompt: Cost in Rs Crores. Includes land acquisition + construction + approvals. Based on developer estimates or audited data.

description: Total saleable area of all units combined (all phases). Represents total marketable area in square feet. Used for area-based metrics, price normalization, and capacity analysis.
sample_prompt: What is the total saleable area in the project?
variation_in_prompt: Show total project area; Total saleable sq.ft.? How big is the entire project? | Tell me the total area available for sale across all units.
assumption_in_prompt: Area = Sum of all unit saleable areas. Measured in square feet. Includes proportionate common area per unit.

description: Total revenue realized from unit sales to date. Includes all booked and completed sales. Critical for cash flow analysis, payment milestone tracking, and project financial health assessment.
sample_prompt: How much revenue has been collected?
variation_in_prompt: Show total sales revenue; How much money collected? Total cash realized? | Tell me the total revenue from selling units in each project.
assumption_in_prompt: Revenue = Sold Units × Average Realized Price. Based on RERA filings or company records. Includes GST.

description: Classification of project by housing type (Residential, Mixed-Use, Commercial). Indicates primary market segment and buyer profile. Important for market comparisons and strategy positioning.
sample_prompt: What is the project category or type?
variation_in_prompt: Show project types; Is it residential or commercial? What kind of project? | Tell me the category of each project (residential, mixed-use, etc.).
assumption_in_prompt: Category = Primary property type. Standard classifications used. One primary category per project.

description: Distribution of unit types in the project (1BHK, 2BHK, 3BHK, etc. percentages or counts). Indicates target buyer segment and market positioning. Important for buyer demographic analysis.
sample_prompt: What is the BHK mix in the project?
variation_in_prompt: Show unit mix; How many 1BHK, 2BHK, 3BHK? What types of units? | Tell me the breakdown of unit types (bedroom count) in each project.
assumption_in_prompt: BHK = Bedroom-Hall-Kitchen configuration. Mix represents as % or count. Reflects market positioning (affordable vs premium).

description: Count of units that have received or are near possession. Indicates project maturity and immediate investment opportunity. Important for inventory analysis and buyer decision-making.
sample_prompt: How many units are ready to occupy?
variation_in_prompt: Show units ready for possession; How many are delivered? RTO units count? | Tell me how many units are ready or soon ready to move in.
assumption_in_prompt: RTO = In possession or within 3 months of possession. Represents deliverable inventory. Does not include defects list items.

description: Count of units still under construction (not yet in defects list stage). Indicates construction progress and remaining project completion timeline. Used for project status assessment.
sample_prompt: How many units are under construction?
variation_in_prompt: Show under construction count; How many units being built? In-progress inventory? | Tell me how many units are still being constructed.
assumption_in_prompt: Under Construction = Structural work ongoing. Does not include completed or possession-ready units. Based on site reports.
formula_derivation: Project Size - RTO Units - Completed Units
